thread.title: Khalid confirms sexuality after being outed says he s not ashamed
thread.title_full: Khalid confirms sexuality after being outed says he s not ashamed

author: Pop Culture & Art
title: Khalid confirms sexuality after being outed says he s not ashamed
text:
Grammy-nominated R&B singer Khalid has publicly come out as gay after being outed on social media.
The 26-year-old artist, known for hits like "Love Lies" and "Young, Dumb, & Broke," shared a series of posts on X (formerly Twitter), confirming his sexuality and emphasizing his self-acceptance.
Posting a rainbow flag with exclamation points, Khalid wrote, “there yall go. next topic please lol.” When a user questioned his post, he replied, “I am [gay]! And that’s okay.” In another tweet, Khalid addressed the situation, writing, “I got outted and the world still continues to turn… I am not ashamed of my sexuality! In reality, it ain’t nobody’s business! But I am okay with me. love yall.”
I got outted and the world still continues to turn. Let’s get this straight (lmao) I am not ashamed of my sexuality! In reality it ain’t nobodies business! But I am okay with me 🖤 love yall
— Khalid (@thegreatkhalid)[November 23, 2024]
🏳️🌈!!!
— Khalid (@thegreatkhalid)
there yall go. next topic please lol[November 22, 2024]
The reveal follows a series of controversial tweets and a photo shared by internet personality Hugo D Almonte, who accused Khalid of lying about their past interactions. Almonte claimed Khalid accused him of breaking into his home and made disparaging comments about him. Almonte’s tweets also included cryptic accusations aimed at unnamed figures in the music industry.
Despite the drama, Khalid has received support from peers. Singer and producer MNEK called out Almonte’s behavior, labeling it “loser behavior” and expressing frustration over the situation.
Khalid has long been private about his personal life, and his coming out marks the first time he has publicly addressed his sexuality. Fans and industry professionals alike continue to rally behind the singer, celebrating his openness and resilience.
